description: Rotation cadence and inactivity-revocation thresholds for IAM credentials.

rotation_targets.preferred_alternative: Managed Identity or Workload Identity Federation.

rotation_targets.rationale: TokenRequest API with audience-bound, time-bounded tokens replaces legacy long-lived SA tokens.

rotation_targets.preferred_alternative: Short-lived SSH certificates issued by an internal CA.

rotation_targets.rationale: CA/Browser Forum baseline limit for publicly-trusted TLS certificates.

anti_patterns.rule: If long-lived credentials exist, an automated rotation job exists (Lambda + scheduled event, GitHub Actions cron, etc.). Manual-only rotation reliably ages out.
Offboarding playbook revokes all credentials within 24 hours. Verify via an IAM credential report diff between before/after.
SSH keys to shared bastion hosts are per-human; do not deploy a shared private key to multiple operators.
Container images / AMIs do not bake an access key into the image (env vars or otherwise). Inject at runtime via secret manager or workload identity.
